title: Platydesma spathulatum
intro: Platydesma spathulatum is a small, shrub-like tree native to the tropical forests of Hawaii and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus family. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Platydesma spathulatum include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Platydesma spathulatum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve cold and flu symptoms, as well as to support immune function.
